2603.18822.title: Detecting Basic Values in A Noisy Russian Social Media Text Data: A Multi-Stage Classification Framework
2603.18822.paper_summary: This study presents a multi-stage classification framework for detecting human values in noisy Russian language social media, validated on a random sample of 7.5 million public text posts. Drawing on Schwartz's theory of basic human values, we design a multi-stage pipeline that includes spam and nonpersonal content filtering, targeted selection of value relevant and politically relevant posts, LLM based annotation, and multi-label classification. Particular attention is given to verifying the quality of LLM annotations and model predictions against human experts. We treat human expert annotations not as ground truth but as an interpretative benchmark with its own uncertainty. To account for annotation subjectivity, we aggregate multiple LLM generated judgments into soft labels that reflect varying levels of agreement. These labels are then used to train transformer based models capable of predicting the probability of each of the ten basic values. The best performing model, XLM RoBERTa large, achieves an F1 macro of 0.83 and an F1 of 0.71 on held out test data. By treating value detection as a multi perspective interpretive task, where expert labels, GPT annotations, and model predictions represent coherent but not identical readings of the same texts, we show that the model generally aligns with human judgments but systematically overestimates the Openness to Change value domain. Empirically, the study reveals distinct patterns of value expression and their co-occurrence in Russian social networks, contributing to a broader research agenda on cultural variation, communicative framing, and value based interpretation in digital environments. All models are released publicly.
2603.18822.paper_summary_zh: 本研究提出了一個多階段分類框架，用於檢測噪聲環境下俄語社交媒體中的人類價值觀，並在750萬條公共文本帖子的隨機樣本上進行驗證。基於施瓦茨的基本人類價值理論，我們設計了一個多階段的流程，包括垃圾郵件和非個人內容過濾、針對性選擇與價值相關和政治相關的帖子、基於LLM的註釋以及多標籤分類。特別注意的是，對LLM註釋和模型預測的質量進行驗證，並與人類專家進行對比。我們將人類專家的註釋視為一種解釋性基準，而非絕對真理，並承認其自身的不確定性。為了考慮註釋的主觀性，我們將多個LLM生成的判斷聚合為反映不同一致性水平的軟標籤。這些標籤隨後用於訓練基於Transformer的模型，能夠預測十個基本價值中的每一個的概率。表現最佳的模型XLM RoBERTa large，在保留的測試數據上達到了0.83的F1宏觀值和0.71的F1值。通過將價值檢測視為一項多角度的解釋性任務，其中專家標籤、GPT註釋和模型預測代表了對同一文本的連貫但不完全相同的解讀，我們展示了該模型通常與人類判斷一致，但系統性地高估了變革開放性價值領域。實證研究揭示了俄羅斯社交網絡中價值表達的不同模式及其共現，為數字環境中的文化變異、傳播框架和基於價值的解釋提供了更廣泛的研究議程。所有模型均已公開發布。
2603.18822.author: Maria Milkova et.al.
